SP Budget Watch
Home Briefings Perspectives Questions Analysis Sources Timeline About
Independent research project — not affiliated with the South Portland School Department or City of South Portland. AI-assisted analysis with full source transparency. Learn more.

Questions People Are Asking

These questions are drawn from what 30 community stakeholders are actually asking about the FY27 school budget. Every question here has at least one answer grounded in public meeting records and budget documents.

Transportation: The Biggest Open Question

27 transportation-related questions, claims, and non-answers have been tracked across the entire budget process. The reconfiguration vote passed — but no attendance boundaries, bus routes, or school assignments exist yet. The superintendent promised a timeline by ~April 10.

See the full transportation analysis →

Programs & Classes 7

What classes and programs are being cut at the middle school and high school? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Is the pre-K expansion protected, or are the staff who run it being cut? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Are positions that support students with disabilities and IEPs being cut? 2 sources · 5 perspectives How does switching to semester-long classes affect a high school student's college application? 2 sources · 1 perspective What classroom supply budgets and non-staff resources are being cut? 2 sources · 1 perspective Who will handle behavior crises at schools if all behavioral support staff are eliminated? 2 sources · 1 perspective What happened to computer science classes at the middle and high school? 2 sources · 1 perspective

How Decisions Get Made 6

Has the district had serious financial audit problems? 2 sources · 2 perspectives Who will be the next superintendent? 2 sources · 1 perspective Why does South Portland spend more per student than similar school districts? 2 sources · 2 perspectives Are the teachers' unions negotiating over how the layoffs are handled? 2 sources · 2 perspectives Is this school year ending in a deficit, and what does that mean for next year? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Will there be a real chance for public input at the April 14 budget workshop? 2 sources · 1 perspective

Equity & Fairness 5

Has the district gotten a legal opinion on whether closing Kayler is allowed under civil rights law? 2 sources · 5 perspectives Are English learner students losing teachers because fewer kids showed up during immigration enforcement? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Are the budget cuts falling harder on schools with more low-income students and students of color? 2 sources · 3 perspectives How is the district making sure federal education money is spent correctly? 2 sources · 2 perspectives Will immigrant students who missed school due to immigration enforcement be counted fairly in enrollment numbers? 2 sources · 3 perspectives

School Closures 4

How much will it cost to bus students to new schools if one elementary school closes? 2 sources · 4 perspectives Did the district study keeping all five elementary schools open instead of closing one? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Has enrollment actually been falling, or has it recently started to level off? 2 sources · 1 perspective Does Skillin Elementary have a large repair bill that could bring it back into the closure conversation? 2 sources · 1 perspective

What Happens Next 4

When will families find out which school their child will attend next year? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Can the district actually get all of this done before school starts in September? 2 sources · 3 perspectives What is the district doing to avoid another budget crisis next year? 2 sources · 2 perspectives What happens if voters reject the school budget in June? 2 sources · 1 perspective

Staffing Cuts 3

Were early retirement offers made to reduce the number of layoffs? 2 sources · 2 perspectives Why are teachers being cut while central office staff seem mostly protected? 2 sources · 3 perspectives Could more layoff notices go out after these 72? 2 sources · 2 perspectives

Tax Impact 1

Have residents been told the full picture of what the sewer rate increases will add to their bills? 2 sources · 4 perspectives

SP Budget Watch — Independent analysis of the South Portland Schools FY27 budget. Built with sourced evidence from public meetings and budget documents.

This is an independent, volunteer project. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by the South Portland School Department or City of South Portland. All analysis is AI-assisted with full source transparency. Learn more about our methodology. | Changelog
